---
title: "Data exploration"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| label: load data


# Using R
# Option 1: tidytuesdayR R package 
install.packages("tidytuesdayR")

tuesdata <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load('2025-06-24')
## OR
# tuesdata <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load(2025, week = 25)

cases_month <- tuesdata$cases_month
cases_year <- tuesdata$cases_year

write.csv(cases_year, "casesdf")

# Option 2: Read directly from GitHub

# cases_month <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-06-24/cases_month.csv')
# cases_year <- readr::read_csv('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-06-24/cases_year.csv')
```

```{r}
#| label: load-packages

library(tidyverse)
library(rsconnect)
library(shiny)
library(leaflet)
library(sf)
# library(leaflet.extras)

```

```{r}
#| label: summary-stats-year

cases_year_counts <- cases_year |>
  count(country) 

summed_cases <- cases_year |>
    group_by(country) |>
      summarise(
        sum_measles = sum(measles_total, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_rubella = sum(rubella_total, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_m_r = sum_measles + sum_rubella,
        sum_discard = sum(discarded_cases, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_total_discard = sum_measles + sum_rubella + sum_discard,
        sum_lab_m = sum(measles_lab_confirmed, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_epi_m = sum(measles_epi_linked, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_clinic_m = sum(measles_clinical, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_lab_r = sum(rubella_lab_confirmed, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_epi_r = sum(rubella_epi_linked, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_clinic_r = sum(rubella_clinical, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_pop_m = sum(measles_incidence_rate_per_1000000_total_population, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_pop_r = sum(rubella_incidence_rate_per_1000000_total_population, na.rm = TRUE),
        sum_pop_mr = sum_pop_r + sum_pop_m
      )

cases_year |>
  summarise(
    min_year = min(year),
    max_year = max(year)
  )

write.csv(summed_cases, file = "summed_cases.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# Get the column names
column_names <- colnames(cases_year)

# Print the result
print(column_names)
# Output: [1] "ID"   "Name" "Age"

```
